*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/ai/compshop/supabase/functions/list-openrouter-models/index.ts
@@
-// Returns ONLY the models the user has enabled on their OpenRouter account.
-// Endpoint: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/user (account-scoped, never the full marketplace).
+// Prefer models enabled on the OpenRouter account. Fall back to the public catalog
+// so admins can still pick a model when account-scoped model listing is unavailable.
*** End Patch

payload.message:
Fixed and deployed.

Live site is now serving `Commit 4fa27c4`.

What changed:
- `list-openrouter-models` now tries account-specific OpenRouter models first.
- If that fails or `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is missing, it returns the public OpenRouter model catalog instead of a non-2xx edge-function failure.
- The Profile card now shows the real fallback note, not “Model list unavailable…”

If you still see the exact old text, your browser/PWA is holding the previous JS bundle. Hard refresh or close/reopen the PWA. After refresh, the dropdown should populate; at worst it may show a note like `OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not configured; showing public OpenRouter models.`

content.text:
Fixed and deployed.

Live site is now serving `Commit 4fa27c4`.

What changed:
- `list-openrouter-models` now tries account-specific OpenRouter models first.
- If that fails or `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is missing, it returns the public OpenRouter model catalog instead of a non-2xx edge-function failure.
- The Profile card now shows the real fallback note, not “Model list unavailable…”

If you still see the exact old text, your browser/PWA is holding the previous JS bundle. Hard refresh or close/reopen the PWA. After refresh, the dropdown should populate; at worst it may show a note like `OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not configured; showing public OpenRouter models.`

payload.last_agent_message:
Fixed and deployed.

Live site is now serving `Commit 4fa27c4`.

What changed:
- `list-openrouter-models` now tries account-specific OpenRouter models first.
- If that fails or `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is missing, it returns the public OpenRouter model catalog instead of a non-2xx edge-function failure.
- The Profile card now shows the real fallback note, not “Model list unavailable…”

If you still see the exact old text, your browser/PWA is holding the previous JS bundle. Hard refresh or close/reopen the PWA. After refresh, the dropdown should populate; at worst it may show a note like `OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not configured; showing public OpenRouter models.`

payload.message:
I tested with a real headless Chromium browser.

Results:
- Production is serving `Commit 4fa27c4 - 2026-07-02 11:19 PM EDT`.
- `/profile` loads correctly, but in a fresh headless browser it redirects/shows the sign-in screen, as expected.
- Screenshot looked normal: CompShop login card with Microsoft, Google, and email/password.
- I could not verify the authenticated admin AI Model dropdown end-to-end because this headless browser has no logged-in admin session or credentials.

So: browser-level production deploy is confirmed. To test the model selector itself with Playwright, I’d need either test login credentials or an authenticated storage/cookie state from an admin session.

content.text:
I tested with a real headless Chromium browser.

Results:
- Production is serving `Commit 4fa27c4 - 2026-07-02 11:19 PM EDT`.
- `/profile` loads correctly, but in a fresh headless browser it redirects/shows the sign-in screen, as expected.
- Screenshot looked normal: CompShop login card with Microsoft, Google, and email/password.
- I could not verify the authenticated admin AI Model dropdown end-to-end because this headless browser has no logged-in admin session or credentials.

So: browser-level production deploy is confirmed. To test the model selector itself with Playwright, I’d need either test login credentials or an authenticated storage/cookie state from an admin session.

payload.last_agent_message:
I tested with a real headless Chromium browser.

Results:
- Production is serving `Commit 4fa27c4 - 2026-07-02 11:19 PM EDT`.
- `/profile` loads correctly, but in a fresh headless browser it redirects/shows the sign-in screen, as expected.
- Screenshot looked normal: CompShop login card with Microsoft, Google, and email/password.
- I could not verify the authenticated admin AI Model dropdown end-to-end because this headless browser has no logged-in admin session or credentials.

So: browser-level production deploy is confirmed. To test the model selector itself with Playwright, I’d need either test login credentials or an authenticated storage/cookie state from an admin session.
